message:
fix(types): explicit source/repair symbol test constructors prevent kind misclassification

Symbol::new_for_test previously inferred SymbolKind from the ESI value
(esi == 0 → Source, else → Repair), which is semantically incorrect —
source vs repair depends on the block's K parameter, not the ESI.
This caused tests that constructed symbols with esi > 0 to inadvertently
create repair symbols when they intended source symbols.

new_for_test now always creates Source symbols (matching the common test
case of constructing ordered source sequences). Two explicit constructors
are added:
- new_source_for_test: always Source
- new_repair_for_test: always Repair

symbol_set tests updated to use new_source_for_test for the existing
test_symbol helper and add a test_repair_symbol helper. New test
verifies repair symbols correctly increment repair_symbols progress
count instead of source_symbols count.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
